section_title: INTRODUCTION AND INITIAL BIAS FOR NQ
content:
Good afternoon, folks, good evening, good morning, depending on where you are. This is a short little
session on NASDAQ.
- Worked with my son today regarding this particular index.
- Shared this morning the draw on liquidity.
- At the time, it favored lower prices.
- Showed a five-handle run on my Twitter feed for Nasdaq, which showed about 20 handles.

Switched over to an intraday Swing model.
- Traded above these relative equal highs.
- Had this Gap, which is a buy side of balance, outside of efficiency.
- The likelihood is that even if it doesn't go all the way through, it's likely to trade into this low and into this
range here.
- The aim was to be very nimble and work with a very tight range.

On the 15-minute time frame:
- This is the old high or relative equal highs that were traded above on Monday.
- The price hugged the current New Week Opening Gap (NWOG).
- Here's the New Week Opening Gap High and New Week Opening Gap Low.
- Price traded up into it here, then moved away.

section_title: MARKET REACTION TO NWOG AND MODEL 2022
content:
Observed a gap on a five-minute chart.
- Noticed separation after trading up into the New Week Opening Gap.
- There was a shift below.
- A rally back up into this area represents the 2022 Model.
- Price then returns back to that very bad Gap.

- CAMERON'S MODEL
The ICT Silver Bullet trade, which is my son Cameron's model.
- This occurs between the 10 o'clock and the 11 o'clock hour.
- It looks for a Fair Value Gap to trade into.

section_title: SILVER BULLET EXECUTION AND INITIAL SCALP
content:
News came out at 10 o'clock, which was high impact.
- Used this order block.
- While inside that order block, went short at 10 o'clock.
- The market broke down, and a small profit was taken to demonstrate a small scalp.
- A five-point scalp on ES is equivalent to about 20 points for a NASDAQ trade.

section_title: SIX SISTER APPROACH AND ES CORRELATION
content:
Noticed the market was likely to continue lower.
- Applied the 'Six Sister Approach' methodology, discussed in recent Twitter spaces.
- This approach helps get in sync with what has already transpired in ES (E-mini S&P 500 futures), which
was looking for lower prices.
- More detailed lessons on the 'Six Sister Approach' will be provided on this YouTube channel.

section_title: IDENTIFYING SELL SIDE LIQUIDITY AND HOURLY IMBALANCE
content:
Looking at the shaded area on a 15-minute time frame, then a 16-minute/hourly chart.
- Identified sell side liquidity.
- There was a small little gap that also resides below that low.
- This was the first imbalance below the sell side liquidity pool.
- The expectation was that price could drop down to this level, using the low to high equilibrium, which
would be below that to reach for the target, making it a sufficient setup.

Dropping down into a one-minute chart to get to the 'nitty-gritty' of the execution.
- The business of the trade is now visible with executions added.

section_title: DETAILED TRADE ENTRY, EXIT, AND NQ/ES EQUIVALENCE
content:
The entry was right inside the order block.
- Entry point was at 13,281.5.
- This entry was inside the five-minute order block.
- Covered the trade as price was running through this low, executing a short-term scalp.
- This trade was the equivalent of a five-handle run on ES for the son.
- The difference when teaching ES versus NQ (NASDAQ) is that a 5-handle move on ES typically equates
to a 20-handle move on NASDAQ.

section_title: MISSED GAP OPPORTUNITY AND SECOND ENTRY
content:
Observed another gap and didn't want to miss it.
- Initially thought price might spike up into it one more time but it never did.
- The subsequent entry was not the best, but it was taken with the expectation of the target being quickly
tagged.
- Treated this area as a breakaway gap, assuming price could have spiked up into it.
- Was fortunate enough to get in on this one right before price wiped out and went lower.

section_title: FINAL TRADE DETAILS AND PROFIT TARGET
content:
This was a small 'city' (setup) that also involved an unbalanced buy-side efficiency.
- Price went up to that point and traded down into the hourly imbalance.
- The high of that move was the limit order for five contracts.
- This resulted in approximately 80 handles on NASDAQ today, which translates to about 40 handles on ES.
